zh.lifeSummary: 當Stephen Randall於1758年10月27日出生在美國緬因州約克貝維其可時，他的父親Eliphalet Randall30歲，母親Lydia Rawlins42歲。他於1781年12月13日與Anna Fogg在美國緬因州約克貝維其可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1837年9月18日在美國緬因州約克利默里克享年78歲。

nameMeanings.name: Randall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): common colloquial pronunciation of   Randolph  .

variants.name: Randell
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Americanized form of German   Randel  .

variants.name: Randon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel.
English: probably a variant of Randall , with assimilation of n-l to n-n.
French (northern): from the ancient Germanic personal name Rando, a short form of names based on the element rand ‘rim (of a shield), shield’.

variants.name: Randol
meanings: English: altered form of   Randall  .

variants.name: Randal
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Randal in Gulen, a compound of Old Norse runnr ‘copse, thicket, grove’ + dalr ‘valley’.

variants.name: Randles
meanings: English and Irish (Louth): variant of Randall , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland this is sometimes a variant of Reynolds .

variants.name: Randle
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Americanized form of German Randel or of its Bavarian variant Randl.

variants.name: Rendell
meanings: English (Somerset, Devon, and Dorset): variant of   Randall  .

variants.name: Randel
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
South German: variant of   Rand   3.

variants.name: Randolph
meanings: English and German: from Randolf, an ancient Germanic personal name composed of the elements rand ‘rim (of a shield), shield’ + wolf ‘wolf’. This was introduced into England by the Normans in Old French forms of two different ancient Germanic personal names which became confused with each other: Randulf (from rand ‘(shield-)edge’ + wulf ‘wolf’) and Rannulf (from hraf(a)n ‘raven’ + wulf ‘wolf’).

History: An American family bearing this surname are descended from William Randolph (c. 1651–1711), a planter and merchant, a member of a family that originally came from Sussex, England. William Randolph emigrated from Warwickshire to VA c. 1673. He was a forebear of Thomas Jefferson and Robert E. Lee. Randolph had seven sons, each of whom inherited an estate, the name of which was sometimes added to their own, such as Sir John Randolph of Tazewell. His great-grandsons included Edmund Randolph (1753–1813), first attorney general of the US and one of the framers of the US Constitution, and the diplomat and statesman John Randolph of Roanoke (1773–1833), who served as US minister to Russia.

variants.name: Rendall
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Scottish: habitational name from Rendall (Orkney), named with a stream-name of uncertain origin + Old Norse dalr ‘valley’.
In some cases possibly also an Americanized form of Swedish   Rendahl  .

nameMeanings.name: Stephen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English spelling of the name of the first Christian martyr (Acts 6–7), whose feast is accordingly celebrated next after Christ's own ( 26 December ). His name is derived from the Greek word stephanos ‘garland, crown’.

variants.name: Stephan
meanings: Variant of   Stephen  , preserving the vowels of the Greek name.

variants.name: Steven
meanings: Variant of   Stephen  , reflecting the normal pronunciation of the name in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries: 1775:  York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1776: York, Massachusetts, United States 1820: York, Maine, United States
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.

historicEvents.title: A Free America
historicEvents.description: The Revolutionary War ended with the signing of the Treaty of Paris which gave the new nation boundries on which they could expand and trade with other countries without any problems.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.

historicEvents.title: Whitehead Light
historicEvents.description: In 1804, President Thomas Jefferson authorized the creation of a light station on Whitehead Island. The light house went into service by 1807. It is the third-oldest light house in Maine. Whitehead Light still exists as the private property of Pine Island Camp, a non-profit organization.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

en.lifeSummary: When Stephen Randall was born on 27 October 1758, in Berwick, York, Maine, United States, his father, Eliphalet Randall, was 30 and his mother, Lydia Rawlins, was 42. He married Anna Fogg on 13 December 1781, in Berwick, York, Maine, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 18 September 1837, in Limerick, York, Maine, United States, at the age of 78.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Stephen Randall est né le 27 octobre 1758, à Berwick, York, Maine, États-Unis, son père, Eliphalet Randall, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lydia Rawlins, avait 42 ans. Il a épousé Anna Fogg le 13 décembre 1781, à Berwick, York, Maine, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 18 septembre 1837, à Limerick, York, Maine, États-Unis, à l’âge de 78 ans.

Dezember 1781 in Berwick, York, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten Anna Fogg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 18. September 1837 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Limerick, York, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten.

ja.lifeSummary: Stephen Randallが1758年10月27日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークバーウィックで生まれた時，父Eliphalet Randallは30歳，母Lydia Rawlinsは42歳であった。1781年12月13日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークバーウィックでAnna Foggと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1837年9月18日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークリメリックで78歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Stephen Randall(이)가 1758년 10월 27일 미국 메인 주 요르크 버윅에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Eliphalet Randall(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lydia Rawlins(은)는 42세였다. 그는 1781년 12월 13일 미국 메인 주 요르크 버윅에서 Anna Fogg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1837년 9월 18일 미국 메인 주 요르크 라임릭에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Stephen Randall родился 27 октября 1758 г. в местности бирвикк, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Eliphalet Randall было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lydia Rawlins, 42. Он женился на женщине по имени Anna Fogg 13 декабря 1781 г. в местности бирвикк, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 18 сентября 1837 г. в местности лимирикк, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 78 лет.
